description: Clean up React anti-patterns and code smells in the browser extension side panel: fix missing button attributes, lift TooltipProvider to shared ancestor, replace hand-rolled dropdown with Radix DropdownMenu, extract repeated setPlugins updater pattern, and deduplicate tool filtering logic.

userStories.title: Fix FailedPluginCard button missing type and cursor-pointer
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the show more/less button in FailedPluginCard to have correct HTML attributes so that it follows accessibility best practices and has proper visual affordance.
acceptanceCriteria: The <button> on line 19 of FailedPluginCard.tsx has type="button" attribute
The <button> has cursor-pointer in its className
userStories.notes: File: platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/FailedPluginCard.tsx, line 19. The button currently has no type attribute (defaults to 'submit' in form contexts) and no cursor-pointer class. Add type="button" and cursor-pointer to the className string. This is a one-line change.

userStories.title: Lift Tooltip.Provider to a shared ancestor instead of per-instance
userStories.description: As a user, I want tooltips to behave correctly when hovering quickly between them (skip delay) so that the UI feels responsive and polished.
acceptanceCriteria: A single Tooltip.Provider wraps the plugin list content (either in App.tsx or PluginList.tsx)
PluginCard.tsx no longer wraps individual tooltips in Tooltip.Provider
ToolRow.tsx no longer wraps individual tooltips in Tooltip.Provider
All existing tooltips still render correctly (version tooltip on PluginIcon, SDK badge tooltip, tool description tooltip)
userStories.notes: Currently Tooltip.Provider is instantiated per-tooltip in PluginCard.tsx (lines 85, 114) and ToolRow.tsx (line 26). Radix documentation recommends a single TooltipProvider wrapping the app or at least the relevant subtree. Multiple providers prevent the delayDuration skip behavior. The fix: add a single <Tooltip.Provider> in App.tsx wrapping the main content area (or the entire return JSX), then remove all individual <Tooltip.Provider> wrappers from PluginCard.tsx and ToolRow.tsx. The Tooltip.Root/Trigger/Content can remain as-is — only the Provider wrapper needs to move up.

userStories.title: Replace hand-rolled AllowAlwaysButton dropdown with Radix DropdownMenu
userStories.description: As a user, I want the 'Allow Always' dropdown in ConfirmationDialog to support keyboard navigation, focus trapping, and Escape-to-close so that the approval flow is fully accessible.
acceptanceCriteria: AllowAlwaysButton uses @radix-ui/react-dropdown-menu instead of manual mousedown outside-click detection
The dropdown supports keyboard navigation (ArrowUp/Down to move between items, Enter to select, Escape to close)
The dropdown still renders the same 2-3 menu items (for this tool on this domain, for this tool everywhere, and conditionally for all tools on domain)
The visual appearance matches the existing neo-brutalist style (border-2 border-border bg-card rounded shadow-md, hover:bg-accent on items)
The manual useEffect with mousedown listener and ref-based outside-click detection is removed
The open/setOpen useState is removed (Radix manages its own open state)
userStories.notes: File: platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/ConfirmationDialog.tsx, lines 57-111 (AllowAlwaysButton component). Currently uses manual state management (open/setOpen), a useRef for outside-click detection, and a useEffect with mousedown listener. Replace with Radix DropdownMenu. @radix-ui/react-dropdown-menu may need to be installed — check package.json first. The project already uses other Radix primitives (accordion, switch, tooltip, slot). Use DropdownMenu.Root, DropdownMenu.Trigger (wrapping the existing Button), DropdownMenu.Portal, DropdownMenu.Content (with the neo-brutalist styling), and DropdownMenu.Item for each option. Apply the same styling classes from the current hand-rolled dropdown to the Radix components. The DropdownMenu.Content should use side="top" and align="end" to match the current positioning (absolute right-0 bottom-full). Make sure to check if @radix-ui/react-dropdown-menu is already a dependency — if not, install it.

userStories.title: Extract repeated setPlugins updater into a helper in PluginCard
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the repeated plugin state update pattern in PluginCard to be extracted into a reusable helper so that the toggle logic is DRY and easier to maintain.
acceptanceCriteria: A helper function (e.g., updatePluginTools) encapsulates the setPlugins(prev => prev.map(p => ...)) pattern
handleToggleAll uses the helper for both optimistic update and rollback
handleToggleTool uses the helper for both optimistic update and rollback
The 4 inline setPlugins calls in PluginCard are replaced by calls to the helper
Behavior is identical (optimistic update, rollback on failure)
userStories.notes: File: platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/PluginCard.tsx. The pattern `setPlugins(prev => prev.map(p => (p.name === plugin.name ? { ...p, tools: <updated> } : p)))` appears 4 times across lines 41-42, 45, 52-55, and 60-64. Extract a helper function inside PluginCard like: `const updatePluginTools = (updater: (tools: ToolState[]) => ToolState[]) => setPlugins(prev => prev.map(p => (p.name === plugin.name ? { ...p, tools: updater(p.tools) } : p)));` Then handleToggleAll becomes: `updatePluginTools(tools => tools.map(t => ({ ...t, enabled: checked })))` and rollback becomes: `updatePluginTools(() => originalTools)`. Similar simplification for handleToggleTool.

userStories.title: Deduplicate tool filtering logic between PluginList and PluginCard
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the tool filtering predicate to be defined once so that PluginList and PluginCard use the same logic without duplication.
acceptanceCriteria: A shared filter predicate function exists (either in a utility file or inline in PluginList)
PluginList.tsx uses the shared predicate to determine which plugins have matching tools
PluginCard.tsx uses the same shared predicate to filter visible tools
The filter logic (matching against displayName, name, and description) is defined exactly once
userStories.notes: PluginList.tsx lines 20-31 filters plugins by checking if any tool matches the filter string against displayName, name, and description (all lowercased). PluginCard.tsx lines 71-79 does the exact same filtering to determine which tools to render. The predicate `(t) => t.displayName.toLowerCase().includes(filterLower) || t.name.toLowerCase().includes(filterLower) || t.description.toLowerCase().includes(filterLower)` is duplicated. Extract a function like `matchesTool(tool: ToolState, filterLower: string): boolean` — could live in a small utility next to bridge.ts or at the top of PluginList.tsx since both PluginList and PluginCard already import from the same area. Then PluginList uses `plugins.filter(p => p.tools.some(t => matchesTool(t, filterLower)))` and PluginCard uses `plugin.tools.filter(t => matchesTool(t, filterLower))`.
